data.title: Canada Carbon schedules annual & special meeting on Jan. 21, 2026 after missing annual meeting since Jan. 31, 2024.
data.description: In Toronto, Ontario, Canada, on Jan. 20, 2026, Canada Carbon Inc. (TSX-V:CCB, FF:U7N1) disclosed plans to hold their annual and special shareholder meeting on January 21, 2026. This meeting comes after failing to conduct an annual shareholder session since January 31, 2024. The decision to convene this meeting aligns with Section 4.1 of Policy 3.2 of the TSX Venture Exchange regulations.

China Vanke Wins Approval for Revised Plan to Extend Yuan Bond China Vanke secured creditor approval to extend repayment on a yuan bond, easing near-term default risk. More than 92% of holders backing the revised plan agreed after the developer added an upfront cash payment.
Platinum climbs to all-time high of $2,483.10 per ounce.
Job posting for federal prosecutor appears in Eastern District of Virginia; Trump loyalist Lindsey Halligan said to hold position-NBC
Daniel Mas appointed mining minister by Chile’s incoming president Kast.
5-year JGB yield eases to 1.680%, down 1.5 bps.
Russian visitors to Japan hit record on easier visa conditions than Europe-NA
Yen Consolidates; Faces Volatility Amid U.S.-Europe Tensions, Japan Fiscal Worries-WSJ
European indices in the futures market decline, Eurostoxx 50 and DAX both down 0.4%.

data.title: Office Properties Income Trust announces 2025 dividend allocations: $0.01 per share ordinary income, record date 01/27/25.
data.description: In Newton, Massachusetts, Office Properties Income Trust (OPI) released details on the dividend allocations for 2025 income tax reporting. The breakdown per OPI Common Share is as follows: Total Unrecaptured Section 897 Dividends - $0.01, Record Dates 01/27/25, Paid/Payable Dates 02/20/25. There are no allocations for Ordinary Income, 199A Dividends, Qualified Dividends, Capital Gain, Section 1250 Gain, or Capital Gain Distribution. This information is crucial for shareholders and investors for tax purposes.
Historic EU-India trade pact near completion, according to Ursula von der Leyen.
Resolution on extending grace period for redeemed “21 Vanke 02” bonds not approved, says China Vanke.
KeyCorp reports Q4 earnings above expectations on strong interest income growth.

data.title: NSG partners with Copyright Alliance to sponsor free webinar on AI and IP, hosted by National Press Foundation.
data.description: A webinar will be presented by the National Press Foundation, centered on legal matters in the field. The event is scheduled for January 20, 2026, in New York. The Next Solutions Group (NSG), a corporate reputation consultancy, has collaborated with the Copyright Alliance to sponsor the series. The webinar is open to all and aims to shed light on the intersection of various legal aspects.

data.title: ChooChoo, an AI reading toy showcased at CES 2026, promotes early childhood language development, gaining media attention.
data.description: In Las Vegas on January 20, 2026, the CES® 2026 event showcased ChooChoo, an interactive reading companion fueled by AI technology. This innovative product aims to aid in early childhood language development and garnered significant interest for its evidence-based method of boosting children's learning abilities.

data.title: Tom Lee from Fundstrat predicts a tough 2026 beginning before a late-year recovery, expecting Bitcoin to reach a new peak.
data.description: Tom Lee, the chairman of Ethereum treasury firm BitMine and a prominent figure at Fundstrat, predicts that Bitcoin will reach a new peak before the end of the year.
